member.name: Irene Campbell
member.role: MP for North Ayrshire and Arran
member.synopsis: Irene Campbell is the Labour MP for North Ayrshire and Arran , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Livestock: Weather

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not hold data on the number of farmed animals that have required veterinary attention and/or died on-farm due to heat stress.Data from 2022 indicates that, in July and August 2022 when there was unprecedented hot weather in the UK, app...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Poultry: Animal Welfare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether her Department has made an assessment of the commercial viability of 'day-zero' systems where genetically edited breeding hens ensure only female layer chicks hatch, eliminatin
writtenQuestions.answerText: As stated in the recently published Animal Welfare Strategy Defra will encourage industry to end the practice of culling male laying hen chicks. In recent years there has been rapid global progress in a range of technologies that could help end the routin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Experiments

writtenQuestions.answerText: The NC3Rs does not sponsor UAR activities beyond being a signatory and participant in the UK Concordat on Openness on Animal Research which is managed by UAR. The NC3Rs works with a range of relevant stakeholders across the life sciences sector, including...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Pigs: Animal Welfare
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to help reduce the prevalence of tail docking on pigs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has recently engaged with the Pig Veterinary Society and the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons to reduce the prevalence of pig tail docking by working with the industry and the veterinary profession. Defra is now carefully considering th...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.answerText: HM Treasury published an updated version of the Green Book on 5th February, which acknowledges the statutory role of the ASC and includes language in paragraph 8.84 noting that appraisals should consider the effects of a proposal on the welfare of animals...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hunting: Animal Welfare

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government will hold a consultation on its plans to ban trail hunting. Defra will be guided by the Government's consultation principles in shaping the proposed consultation. The department plans to introduce legislation to ban the activity of trail hu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Ammunition: Factories
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to build energetics and munitions factories.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have committed £1.5 billion to move munitions production to “always on” and build six new munitions and energetics factories. Work is underway to plan the new factories.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Credit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, with reference to the Financial Inclusion Strategy, published on 5 November, whether the Government plans to develop targets as well as metrics to monitor progress in delivering the Strategy’s objectives on improvin
writtenQuestions.answerText: Earlier this month, I published the Government’s Financial Inclusion Strategy setting out an ambitious programme of measures to improve financial inclusion and resilience for underserved groups across the UK. The Strategy was developed alongside a committ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Banks: Credit
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what steps she will take to ensure banks provide access to affordable credit to people who are financially excluded.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that, when provided responsibly, credit can play an important role in helping people manage unexpected expenses and smooth their cash flow. Access to suitable, affordable credit products can support people’s financial resilience ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Hunting
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that trail hunting is not used for the illegal hunting of wild mammals with dogs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government is committed to enacting a ban on trail hunting in line with our manifesto commitment. Work to determine the best approach for doing so is ongoing and Defra will consult on how to deliver a ban in the new year.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Credit Unions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what recent progress her Department has made in reviewing responses to the call for evidence on reforming the credit union common bond in Great Britain; and when her Department plans to publish its proposed next ste
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises the role that credit unions play in providing savings and affordable loans to their members, serving local communities throughout the country. This is why the government is taking steps to ensure credit unions are fully supported...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Welfare: UK Trade with EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what discussions she has had with EU negotiators on the inclusion of UK animal welfare standards within the proposed UK-EU Sanitary and Phytosanitary Agreement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We will agree a Sanitary and Phytosanitary (SPS) Agreement to make agrifood trade with our biggest market cheaper and easier, cutting costs and red tape for British producers and retailers, and helping to reduce the pressure on prices and increase choice ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Products: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether he plans to introduce mandatory method-of-production labelling for animal products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 12 June 2025, we published the summary of responses and the Government response to the fairer food labelling consultation, which was undertaken last year by the previous Government. The response is available here on GOV.UK. We will consider the potenti...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Poultry: Animal Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps his Department is taking to end the use of enriched cages for hens in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the reply previously given to the hon. Member for Liverpool West Derby, Ian Byrne, on 16 May 2025, PQ 50228.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Cats and Dogs: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many (a) dogs and (b) cats were imported non commercially under the PETS Directive in each month of 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 2024, a total of 335451 dogs and 32804 cats were imported non-commercially through the PETS Directive. The table below shows the number of dogs and cats imported each month in 2024: 2024CatsDogsTotal number of animalsJanuary32073035833565February186015...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Cats and Dogs: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many (a) dogs and (b) cats were imported under commercial rules under the Ballai Directive in each month of 2024; and from which country.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In 2024, a total of 32391 dogs and 6226 cats were imported commercially under the Balai directive. The attached two tables show all commercial imports of dogs and cats by country of origin and month of import. The EU data may include animals that have ori...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

items.text: Name of donor: The Vegetarian Society Address of donor: 15 Jersey Street, Ancoats, Manchester M4 6EZ Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights to and from Amsterdam (£489.78), train tickets between Schipol airport and Amsterdam, Amsterdam and Eindhoven, Eindhoven and Schipol airport (£54.98), hotel accommodation (£129.03) and travel insurance (£1.46), value £675.25 Destination of visit: Netherlands (Amsterdam and Eindhoven) Dates of visit: 18 January 2026 to 19 January 2026 Purpose of visit: As part of the Vegetarian Society Hatch and Despatch campaign I visited a facility to see how in ovo sexing in eggs works to avoid killing male chicks at birth. (Registered 4 February 2026)
Chair of Splash Group. This is an unpaid role. (Registered 2 August 2024)
